issues: '有一位小姐請我喝茶，問我喝不喝...我不喜歡咖啡，我喜歡喝茶' — clones R1's tea-vs-coffee/nationality beats almost intact
'周先生謝謝林小姐來接他們' — 'them' has no antecedent, only 周先生 was picked up (R1 leftover)
airport-pickup+tea-offer+nationality-question sequence duplicates R1 near-verbatim, third telling of same event
'周老師的爸爸也是老師，很喜歡看書，所以他們家有很多書' clones R2's father-teacher-loves-books-house-full-of-books beat
favorite-family-photo climax duplicates R2's photo-hobby beat almost point for point
'台北最有名的小吃就是牛肉麵，所以他每個星期都要到一家老店去吃' duplicates R1's famous-noodle-shop premise
'現在都住在山上的學校宿舍裡...再一起坐火車去花蓮' — already living there vs. traveling there, unresolved contradiction
'因為下午一點我得去銀行' duplicates R1's identical '一點...去銀行' detail almost verbatim
'晚上，一個朋友打電話來，問我後天有沒有空...去唱歌' reuses R1's 後天-KTV call setup
bank visit ends 1:30pm but text claims 'almost late' for a class starting 2pm — internal timeline error

Recycles both 臺東 (R1's destination) and 貓空 (R2's destination) instead of a new scene: 我最喜歡臺東，那裡的海很美。我還去過貓空，在山上的茶館喝茶 — the interview should have surfaced a different situation, not summarized the other two articles.
Templated phrase reuse: R1 '這種芒果又香又甜...我以前沒吃過這麼好吃的西瓜' vs R3 '芒果又香又甜，我以前沒吃過這麼好吃的芒果' — same sentence skeleton, object swapped, not organic variation.
'念到很累' collocation ('念書真的需要花很多時間，我每天都念到很累') is reused verbatim in R2 and R3 — templated, not organic language.
Life arc (worried about tuition, hoping for a scholarship, ambiguous company-job-vs-small-business future) is the same arc repeated in R2 and R3.
Repeats '念到很累' verbatim: 每天念到很累，常常想.
Near-verbatim with R3 on career ambition: R2 '或者自己做一點小生意' vs R3 '或者再做一點自己的小生意'.
Third verbatim occurrence of '念到很累': 有時候她念到很累，可是她沒有放棄.
Retells the same study-Chinese/scholarship-worry/vague-career arc as R1 and R2 with no distinguishing situation.

Diary retells R1's exact surprise-party event from the recipient's POV — explicitly banned 'diary-of-R1' pattern. Same characters (高美 orders the cake, 林小姐 hands over a gift) and the identical line 祝你萬事如意，心想事成 verbatim from R1.
Plot inconsistency on top of the reuse: R1 says the party is at 高's house ('就在我家'), but R3's diarist returns to her own home ('一到家門口') to find everyone waiting.

Duplicates R1's exact scene mechanism (companion notices bad complexion, asks 是不是生病了, touches the sick person's forehead, escorts to health center): R1 '你今天的臉色很難看，怎麼了？是不是生病了？' vs R3 '覺得他的臉色很難看，就問他...你怎麼了？是不是生病了？'.
Register slip: 高先生 works at a company ('高先生在一家公司上班') but is taken to '健康中心' — a term R1/R2 establish as a SCHOOL health center; a working adult would go to a 診所 or 醫院, not a campus clinic.
Same-event-retold: reuses R1's exact direction template ('看見郵局以後左轉，走進旁邊那條小巷子，麵店就到了' mirrors R1's 'you右轉…看見師大的大門' sequence) with 高同學 subbing for 高先生.
Duplicates R2's convenience-store detail near-verbatim: R2 '又順手買了一枝筆和一本本子…正好門口就有一台提款機' vs R3 '裡面有提款機…買了一枝新的筆和一本本子'.
All 3 articles orbit the same 和平東路/師大路/麵店 landmarks — thin scene variety, echoes the prior B1L01 scene-variety=false flag.
Primary speaker introduced as '我是語言系的黃主任' — surname 黃 is outside the approved 甲乙丙丁/高林周 set.
First-person narrator self-names '我叫陳美玲' — surname 陳 unapproved, and 美玲/月美 is a near-collision with the explicitly banned textbook character 陳月美.
Central interviewee named '張師父，您在這個體育館教太極拳已經三十年了' — surname 張 not in the approved 甲乙丙丁/高林周 set, and unlike R1's passing mention this is the article's main speaking character.
Third repetition in one lesson of the same fitness-transformation beat ('有的人本來連一百公尺都走不動，現在天天早上自己起來運動').
Entire article uses ASCII halfwidth punctuation instead of fullwidth — e.g. 「我的手機呢?剛剛還在這裡,怎麼一下子就不見了?」
「(過了一會兒)」 uses ASCII parentheses, not fullwidth （）
Entire article uses ASCII halfwidth commas throughout instead of fullwidth，
「檢查手機裡的功能通知」 — stiff, over-technical phrasing ('function notifications') for a first-person monologue register
Entire article uses ASCII halfwidth punctuation (, ? ;) throughout instead of fullwidth
Entire article uses ASCII halfwidth punctuation, e.g. 「張伯伯,新年快樂!歡迎歡迎,請進,別客氣。」
張伯伯's turns chain into back-to-back customs lectures ('圍爐的意思是說...', '年糕代表步步高升，橘子代表吉祥...') cued by a child's leading questions — reads as an exposition dump, not real dialogue
Entire article uses ASCII halfwidth punctuation throughout, not fullwidth — otherwise the strongest, most natural piece in the lesson
Entire article uses ASCII halfwidth punctuation throughout, not fullwidth

Profiles real living celebrity 吳寶春 with his actual biography (father died young, mother raised the family, Taipei bakery apprenticeship, 2008 Singapore competition, world bread championship) — violates corpus's 'original only' copyright rule; recounting a real identifiable person's real life story risks close-paraphrase of existing published bios and factual/defamation risk, unlike the corpus's invented-character norm.

Third and weakest repeat of the '林-surnamed elder, 三十幾年, small food business, 人情味' template already used in B3L04 R2 and B3L09 R2 — nearly identical opening formula ('我姓林,大家都叫我林太太,今年六十五歲,在巷口擺攤子賣中式早餐已經三十幾年了') and closing sentiment about the lane's warmth.
免得 used 6× in one dialogue (被擋在門口/遲到/響起來/影響觀眾/顯得隨便/又錯過) — 5-word problem: drilled, not conversational
「去年得獎的那位導演，演技跟劇情的安排都很特別」 — 5-word problem: 演技 wrongly describes a director
「曾經坐在我旁邊的人手機一直響」 — 5-word problem: 曾經 forced into odd modifier slot
No news lead/dateline; opens 「科技日新月異，徹底改變了人們的生活方式」 — 5-word problem: generic essay, not a report
Reporter framing (記者採訪了幾位家長) only appears in paragraph 6 — weak 報導 execution vs. sibling lessons' R3s
